name: acme-design-system
description: The machine-checkable form of guidelines.md — one policy per guideline section, kept right next to it so you can see how they relate. Each policy's `sql` selects violation rows directly from the fact tables (SELECT * ... WHERE ...; zero rows = pass) — facts are compared to policies with no reference/allowed-set layer in between. `message` is a template whose {placeholders} are filled from the matched row's columns. How the prose becomes SQL (hand-authored or LLM-compiled behind a deterministic gate) is DOWNSTREAM.md §5; this file is the compiled result.

policies.sql: SELECT * FROM css_declaration WHERE value_type = 'color' AND certainty = 'literal'
policies.message: Hardcoded color {norm} on "{property}" — reference a color token instead
policies.help: A literal color is the violation: a conformant value is a token reference (var(--color-*), a token constant), which factlas records as a keyword/dynamic fact, not a literal color. Unresolved values are routed to needs-review, never failed here.

policies.sql: SELECT * FROM css_declaration WHERE value_type = 'length' AND certainty = 'literal'
policies.message: Hardcoded length {norm} on "{property}" — use a spacing/radius token instead
policies.help: A literal length is the violation: reference a spacing or radius token (var(--space-*), var(--radius-*)) rather than a raw pixel value.

policies.message: Arbitrary Tailwind class "{token}" bypasses the token scale
policies.help: Replace bracketed arbitrary values (e.g. text-[#123456], w-[13px]) with a scale utility.

policies.message: Inline style sets "{property}"; prefer a class or token
policies.help: Move inline styles into a stylesheet or a token-driven class.

policies.sql: SELECT * FROM css_declaration WHERE certainty IN ('dynamic', 'unknown')
policies.help: factlas could not resolve this value statically; a human or a gated Tier-2 check should decide. Never silently passed or failed.
